newsSince: Arista launched its 1.6T 7060XE7 portfolio for AI fabrics on June 9, with endorsements from Meta, Microsoft (Azure Maia), and Oracle and an AMD scale-out collaboration — 100 Tb/s aggregate and ~60% lower interconnect power via Linear Pluggable Optics. This confirms/extends the AI-Ethernet thesis already cited in the prior 'up' case. Stock +7.6% since June 9.

sources.label: Arista raises AI Ethernet outlook (Simply Wall St)
Arista doubles 2026 AI target (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: ANET's AI-networking thesis is strong but the catalysts (raised AI Ethernet guide, 1.6T launch) are already known and were captured pre-June 9. The +7.6% bounce removes some of the asymmetry the prior case credited. It remains a $207B widely-owned benchmark far from the passive/power core. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Open-Ethernet share gains as AI clusters shift off InfiniBand; AI revenue more than doubling in 2026.
verdicts.neutral: Catalyst already priced; name sits far from this page's passive/power bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Heavily owned $207B benchmark with Meta/Microsoft concentration; little residual alpha here.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI Ethernet revenue ramp toward the raised ~$3.25B target.

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck list, optical/high-speed-networking bucket: the prior 'up' premise is now confirmed — the 1.6T 7060XE7 launch (Jun 9) with Meta/Microsoft/Oracle/AMD endorsements strengthens the scale-out bandwidth/optics thesis. Fundamental support firmer; sustain the up-move.
verdicts.bull: 1.6T rack-scale systems with LPO and broad hyperscaler endorsement deepen scale-out fabric leadership.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis confirmed rather than transformed; demand was already expected.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 1.6T AI-Ethernet ramp and AMD scale-out NIC collaboration
